rules.title: Namespace dependency cycles should be avoided
Structs with only immutable state should be declared readonly
Methods marked pure must not mutate observable state
Side-effect-free computations should be marked pure
Native methods container should be internal and static
Platform invoke declarations should live in a dedicated native methods class
Classes should not sit too deep in the inheritance hierarchy

parameters.name: maxdepth
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 5.

rules.title: Types should not declare too many fields

parameters.name: maxfields
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 15.

rules.title: Types should not hold too much instance state

parameters.name: maxinstancefields
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 15.

rules.title: Types should not declare too many methods

parameters.name: maxmethods
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 20.

rules.title: Methods should not have too many overloads

parameters.name: maxoverloads
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 6.

rules.title: Methods should not declare too many parameters

parameters.name: maxparameters
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 7.

rules.title: Types should not reference too many other types

parameters.name: maxcoupling
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 20.

rules.title: Fields should not be assigned from too many methods

parameters.name: maxassigningmethods
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 3.

rules.title: Methods should not be hard to maintain

parameters.name: maxcomplexity
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 15.

rules.title: Interfaces should not declare too many members

parameters.name: maxmembers
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 20.

rules.title: Types should not be too long

parameters.name: maxlines
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 400.

rules.title: Field names should not be excessively long

parameters.name: maxnamelength
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 40.

rules.title: Methods should not be too long

parameters.name: maxstatements
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 40.

rules.title: Method names should not be excessively long

parameters.name: maxnamelength
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 40.

rules.title: Type names should not be excessively long

parameters.name: maxnamelength
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 40.

rules.title: Types should not expose too many mutable reference-typed fields

parameters.name: maxmutablecomplexfields
parameters.description: Threshold the rule compares against; measurements above it are reported. Default: 0.

rules.title: Types with poor cohesion should be split
Abstract base classes should carry a 'Base' suffix

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: Value the rule applies when evaluating code. Default: Base.

rules.title: Attribute types should carry an 'Attribute' suffix
Public collection members should have a plural name
Controller types should carry a 'Controller' suffix
Types in the same directory should share one namespace
Exception types should carry an 'Exception' suffix
Private instance fields should start with an underscore
Interface names should begin with the letter 'I'
An interface's 'I' prefix should be followed by an uppercase letter
Methods in a type should not differ only by letter case
Method names should start with an uppercase letter
Namespace names should match the source file folder path
Types in the same namespace should live in the same directory
Private static fields should use an 's_' prefix
Types should not share a name with their containing namespace
Type names should start with an uppercase letter
Abstract type constructors should be protected or private
Constructors of abstract types should not be public
Exceptions should not derive from ApplicationException
Argument exceptions should name a real parameter
Assemblies should not be referenced at multiple versions
Annotate attribute types with AttributeUsage
Attribute types should be sealed
Attributes should restrict the targets they apply to
Base classes should not reference their derived types
Boxing and unboxing conversions should be avoided
Catch clauses should name an exception type
Small immutable value holders are candidates to become structs
Avoid implementing ICloneable
Public members should return a collection abstraction
Avoid exposing concrete XmlNode return types
Parsing of numbers and dates should specify a culture
Prefer Func and Action over custom delegate types
Dangerous thread control methods should not be used
Constructed exceptions should be thrown, not discarded
Types holding disposable fields should be disposable
Disposable fields should be disposed
Methods named Dispose should belong to a disposable type
Dispose should suppress finalization
Types should not be defined in more than one assembly
Distinct types should not share the same simple name
Catch blocks should not be empty
Empty finalizers should be removed
Marker interfaces with no members should be avoided
Documentation comments should not have an empty summary
Remove empty static constructors
Events should be raised through a null-conditional invocation
Prefer task-based concurrency over manual thread creation
Fields should be assigned only within their declaring hierarchy
Mark fields readonly when they are never reassigned
Fields should not hide a field of the same name in a base type
Code should not force garbage collection
GC.Collect should be followed by GC.WaitForPendingFinalizers
General exception types should not be thrown
Members should not hide inherited members
Make methods static when they use no instance state
Prefer generic collections over Hashtable and ArrayList
Avoid List.Contains lookups inside loops
Monitor acquisition and release should be paired in one method
Each source file should declare a single top-level type
Collection properties should be read-only
Record properties should not have public setters
Structures should be immutable
Nested type members should not mask an outer static member
Method that does not use instance state should be static
Declare exception types as public
Static fields should be read-only
Declare enums with the default Int32 underlying type
Methods should not throw NotImplementedException
Obsolete APIs should not be referenced
Nested type should not be more accessible than its container
Overrides of Dispose(bool) should call the base implementation
Hard-coded paths should not climb above the working directory
Property getters should not modify object state
Lower the visibility of protected fields in sealed types
Lower the visibility of protected methods in sealed types
Protected nested type in a sealed class should be private
Public constants should be replaced with static read-only fields
Event handler methods should not be public
Keep event handler methods non-public
Avoid exposing fields with public or internal visibility
Members should not be more accessible than their containing type
Platform invoke declarations should not be publicly visible
Native handles should not be exposed as public fields
Readonly array fields can still be mutated
ReaderWriterLock acquire and release should be paired in one method
Readonly fields of mutable reference types give a false sense of immutability
Fold Where into the following LINQ operator
Avoid reflection calls inside loops
Enum members should not be named Reserved
Reserved exception types should not be thrown
Methods implementing a non-public interface should be sealed
Non-extensible classes should be sealed
ISerializable implementers should carry SerializableAttribute
Serializable types must provide a deserialization constructor
The singleton pattern should be avoided
Types with only static members should be static
Static fields should not be assigned from instance methods
Static fields should not hold mutable collection types
Avoid string concatenation inside loops
Composite format strings should match their argument list
Switch statements should declare a default clause
ThreadStatic should only be applied to static fields
Static constructors should not throw
Methods prefixed with Try should return bool
Types should be declared inside a namespace
Dispose objects a method creates and owns
Newly created disposables should not be discarded
Remove or make static classes that can never be instantiated
Types owning unmanaged resources should declare a finalizer
Private method has an unused parameter
Private field is never used
Private method is never used
Private nested type is never used
URI and URL members should be typed as System.Uri
Use nameof for parameter names in argument exceptions
Use var when the type is evident from a constructor call
Structs that override Equals should define the equality operator
Constructors should not call overridable members
Constructors should not read overridable properties
Avoid publicly visible nested types
XML doc should not describe a nonexistent parameter
Types should not derive from XmlDocument
Certificate validation must not be disabled
COM security initialization calls should be reviewed
Data adapter queries should not be built by string concatenation
System.Random should not produce security-sensitive values
IP address literals should be valid
JSON string literals should be well-formed
Regular expression literals should compile
Absolute URI literals should be valid
Database command text should not be built by string concatenation
Static initialization cycles should be avoided
Weak block ciphers such as DES and Triple DES should not be used
Asynchronous methods should carry an 'Async' name suffix
Synchronous methods should not carry an 'Async' name suffix
Conditional expressions yielding boolean literals should be simplified
Boolean expressions should not be compared to a boolean literal
Boolean results should be returned directly instead of through if/else
Commented-out code should be removed
if/else assigning one variable should use the conditional operator
Composite formatting in console output should use string interpolation
Empty object or collection initializers should be removed
Emptiness checks should not compare a string to an empty literal
Properties that only wrap a backing field should be auto-implemented
Single-statement members should use an expression body
Extension methods should be called through member access
Private fields assigned only during construction should be readonly
Fields should be declared before other members
Read-only properties over a backing field should be getter-only auto-properties
Long if/else-if chains testing one value should use a 'switch'
Index-based 'for' loops over a collection should prefer 'foreach'
Local variables that never change should be declared const
Counting loops should bound the counter with a relational comparison
Nested if statements without an else should be combined
Awaited tasks in reusable code should specify ConfigureAwait
Mutable value types should be reference types
A negated 'All' query should be written as 'Any'
A negated 'Any' query should be written as 'All'
Null guards around a single call should use the null-conditional operator
Long numeric literals should use digit separators
Object creation followed by property assignments should use an object initializer
Prefer 'string.Empty' over an empty string literal
String literals with only escaped backslashes should be verbatim
Auto-property setters assigned only in a constructor should be private
PropertyChangedEventArgs should be cached rather than allocated per notification
Fields should not be explicitly initialized to their default value
Redundant 'else' after a terminating branch should be removed
Lambdas that only forward their arguments should be method groups
Redundant 'ToString()' in string concatenation should be removed
Compound 'if' conditions joined by '&&' may read better as nested ifs
Repeated 'Regex.IsMatch' with an inline pattern should use a cached Regex
'string.Format' calls should prefer string interpolation
Switch sections should be enclosed in braces
Lines should not end with trailing whitespace
Unnecessary parentheses should be removed
Verbatim strings without escapes should be regular strings
Constructor parameters should be used
Unused private methods should be removed
Empty Unity lifecycle methods should be removed
Frame updates should use Time.deltaTime not Time.fixedDeltaTime
InitializeOnLoadMethod targets must be static and parameterless
Types marked InitializeOnLoad require a static constructor
Prefer the generic GetComponent overload
Prefer the 32-bit pixel API over SetPixels
SerializeField is redundant on a public field
Instantiate ScriptableObject types with CreateInstance
